vertica: CREATE LOCAL TEMP TABLE stage_alias_check ON COMMIT PRESERVE ROWS AS SELECT CAST(created_at AS DATE), order_id FROM analytics_src.orders_daily;
trino: DROP TABLE IF EXISTS stage_alias_check; CREATE TABLE stage_alias_check AS SELECT CAST(created_at AS DATE) AS created_date, order_id FROM analytics_src.orders_daily;
context_hint: Use a named expression inside CTAS when a missing column name error points to an unnamed CAST expression.
notes: missing column name ctas alias repair named cast expression

vertica: CREATE LOCAL TEMP TABLE stage_amount_fix ON COMMIT PRESERVE ROWS AS SELECT amount::numeric(18,2), order_id FROM analytics_src.order_amounts;
trino: DROP TABLE IF EXISTS stage_amount_fix; CREATE TABLE stage_amount_fix AS SELECT CAST(amount AS DECIMAL(18,2)) AS amount_value, order_id FROM analytics_src.order_amounts;
context_hint: Name converted numeric expressions in CTAS output.
notes: missing column name decimal alias repair

vertica: SELECT order_id, amount::numeric(18,2) total_amount FROM analytics_src.orders;
trino: SELECT order_id, CAST(amount AS DECIMAL(18,2)) AS total_amount FROM analytics_src.orders;
context_hint: Alias converted expressions explicitly.
notes: expression alias projection naming

vertica: CREATE LOCAL TEMP TABLE stage_event_day ON COMMIT PRESERVE ROWS AS SELECT event_time::date, user_id FROM analytics_src.user_events;
trino: DROP TABLE IF EXISTS stage_event_day; CREATE TABLE stage_event_day AS SELECT CAST(event_time AS DATE) AS event_day, user_id FROM analytics_src.user_events;
context_hint: Use a semantic alias when converting timestamp-to-date expressions.
notes: missing column name event day alias

vertica: SELECT user_id, revenue / 100 AS revenue_major FROM analytics_src.user_revenue;
trino: SELECT user_id, CAST(revenue AS DECIMAL(18,2)) / 100 AS revenue_major FROM analytics_src.user_revenue;
context_hint: Preserve named derived expressions.
notes: derived metric alias preserved

vertica: SELECT user_id, CASE WHEN status_id IN (1,2,3) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS is_active, is_active * weight AS weighted_active FROM analytics_src.user_status_daily;
trino: SELECT q.user_id, q.is_active, q.is_active * q.weight AS weighted_active FROM (SELECT user_id, CASE WHEN status_id IN (1,2,3) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS is_active, weight FROM analytics_src.user_status_daily) q;
context_hint: If a same SELECT alias is reused inside another expression, materialize it in a subquery.
notes: same select alias reuse derived column subquery

vertica: SELECT order_id, seller_id || '_' || campaign_id AS seller_campaign_key, seller_campaign_key || '_v2' AS extended_key FROM analytics_src.orders;
trino: SELECT q.order_id, q.seller_campaign_key, q.seller_campaign_key || '_v2' AS extended_key FROM (SELECT order_id, CAST(seller_id AS VARCHAR) || '_' || CAST(campaign_id AS VARCHAR) AS seller_campaign_key FROM analytics_src.orders) q;
context_hint: Avoid alias reuse in the same SELECT by using an inner query.
notes: same select alias reuse string key derived column

vertica: CREATE LOCAL TEMP TABLE stage_ratio_fix ON COMMIT PRESERVE ROWS AS SELECT amount / quantity, item_id FROM analytics_src.order_items;
trino: DROP TABLE IF EXISTS stage_ratio_fix; CREATE TABLE stage_ratio_fix AS SELECT amount / NULLIF(quantity, 0) AS unit_amount, item_id FROM analytics_src.order_items;
context_hint: Missing column name in CTAS can be fixed by aliasing a derived expression.
notes: missing column name ctas alias derived ratio

vertica: SELECT account_id, ZEROIFNULL(balance) FROM analytics_src.account_balance;
trino: SELECT account_id, COALESCE(balance, 0) AS balance_safe FROM analytics_src.account_balance;
context_hint: Replace Vertica-specific null helpers and keep explicit aliases.
notes: null helper alias normalization

vertica: SELECT user_id, event_time::date AS event_day FROM analytics_src.user_events WHERE event_day >= :first_date::date;
trino: SELECT q.user_id, q.event_day FROM (SELECT user_id, CAST(event_time AS DATE) AS event_day FROM analytics_src.user_events) q WHERE q.event_day >= CAST(:first_date AS DATE);
context_hint: Move alias-dependent filters into an outer query.
notes: alias scope outer query date filter

vertica: SELECT order_id, DECODE(status_code, 1, 'new', 2, 'done', 'other') FROM analytics_src.orders;
trino: SELECT order_id, CASE WHEN status_code = 1 THEN 'new' WHEN status_code = 2 THEN 'done' ELSE 'other' END AS status_label FROM analytics_src.orders;
context_hint: Translate DECODE into CASE with an explicit alias.
notes: decode case alias normalization

vertica: SELECT item_id, amount::numeric(20,2), amount::numeric(20,2) * vat_rate AS amount_with_vat FROM analytics_src.items;
trino: SELECT q.item_id, q.amount_value, q.amount_value * q.vat_rate AS amount_with_vat FROM (SELECT item_id, CAST(amount AS DECIMAL(20,2)) AS amount_value, vat_rate FROM analytics_src.items) q;
context_hint: Materialize a reused cast expression in an inner query.
notes: same select alias reuse numeric cast

vertica: TRUNCATE TABLE target_schema.daily_snapshot; INSERT INTO target_schema.daily_snapshot SELECT * FROM stage_snapshot;
trino: DROP TABLE IF EXISTS target_schema.daily_snapshot_trino; CREATE TABLE target_schema.daily_snapshot_trino AS SELECT * FROM stage_snapshot;
context_hint: For an unpartitioned refresh, recreate the target with CTAS instead of truncate plus insert.
notes: unpartitioned overwrite recreate truncate ctas reload

vertica: DELETE FROM target_schema.session_rollup WHERE event_date >= :first_date::date; INSERT INTO target_schema.session_rollup SELECT * FROM stage_rollup;
trino: CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE target_schema.session_rollup_trino AS SELECT * FROM stage_rollup;
context_hint: Use full-table rebuild only for an unpartitioned refresh target.
notes: unpartitioned refresh rebuild create or replace

vertica: CREATE LOCAL TEMP TABLE stage_summary ON COMMIT PRESERVE ROWS AS SELECT user_id, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM analytics_src.user_events GROUP BY 1;
trino: DROP TABLE IF EXISTS stage_summary; CREATE TABLE stage_summary AS SELECT user_id, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM analytics_src.user_events GROUP BY 1;
context_hint: Temp-table CTAS becomes DROP TABLE IF EXISTS plus CREATE TABLE AS.
notes: temp table ctas normalization

vertica: SELECT event_date FROM analytics_src.sales_events WHERE event_date::date >= '2024-01-01'::date AND event_date::date < :last_date::date;
trino: SELECT event_date FROM analytics_src.sales_events WHERE CAST(event_date AS DATE) >= DATE '2024-01-01' AND CAST(event_date AS DATE) < CAST(:last_date AS DATE);
context_hint: Use typed DATE literals and cast colon parameters explicitly.
notes: typed date literal casted parameter

vertica: SELECT user_id, event_time, ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY event_time DESC) AS rn FROM analytics_src.user_events QUALIFY rn = 1;
trino: SELECT q.user_id, q.event_time, q.rn FROM (SELECT user_id, event_time, ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY event_time DESC) AS rn FROM analytics_src.user_events) q WHERE q.rn = 1;
context_hint: QUALIFY must be rewritten through a subquery or CTE.
notes: qualify to subquery window filter

vertica: INSERT INTO target_schema.sales_snapshot SELECT :launch_id AS launch_id, :version_id AS version_id, event_date::date AS event_date, amount FROM analytics_src.sales_snapshot_src GROUP BY 1,2,3,4 ORDER BY 4;
trino: INSERT INTO target_schema.sales_snapshot_trino SELECT CAST(:launch_id AS BIGINT) AS launch_id, CAST(event_date AS DATE) AS event_date, amount FROM analytics_src.sales_snapshot_src GROUP BY 1,2,3 ORDER BY 3;
context_hint: If version_id is removed, renumber positional GROUP BY and ORDER BY references that followed it.
notes: launch_id version_id group by 3 order by 4 renumber

vertica: INSERT INTO target_schema.metric_rollup SELECT :launch_id AS launch_id, :version_id AS version_id, seller_id, SUM(amount) AS total_amount FROM analytics_src.metric_rollup_src GROUP BY 1,2,3;
trino: INSERT INTO target_schema.metric_rollup_trino SELECT CAST(:launch_id AS BIGINT) AS launch_id, seller_id, SUM(amount) AS total_amount FROM analytics_src.metric_rollup_src GROUP BY 1,2;
context_hint: After version_id removal, keep launch_id and renumber grouped ordinals.
notes: launch_id version_id group by renumber aggregate

vertica: INSERT INTO target_schema.user_daily_metrics SELECT :launch_id AS launch_id, :version_id AS version_id, user_id, event_time::date AS event_date, amount::numeric(18,2) AS amount FROM analytics_src.user_daily_metrics_src GROUP BY 1,2,3,4,5;
trino: INSERT INTO target_schema.user_daily_metrics_trino SELECT CAST(:launch_id AS BIGINT) AS launch_id, user_id, CAST(event_time AS DATE) AS event_date, CAST(amount AS DECIMAL(18,2)) AS amount FROM analytics_src.user_daily_metrics_src GROUP BY 1,2,3,4;
context_hint: Preserve launch_id, drop version_id, and renumber positional groupings.
notes: launch_id version_id group by renumber public metrics

vertica: INSERT INTO target_schema.user_daily_metrics SELECT :launch_id AS launch_id, :version_id AS version_id, user_id, event_time::date AS event_date, amount::numeric(18,2) AS amount FROM analytics_src.user_daily_metrics_src ORDER BY 1,2,3,4,5;
trino: INSERT INTO target_schema.user_daily_metrics_trino SELECT CAST(:launch_id AS BIGINT) AS launch_id, user_id, CAST(event_time AS DATE) AS event_date, CAST(amount AS DECIMAL(18,2)) AS amount FROM analytics_src.user_daily_metrics_src ORDER BY 1,2,3,4;
context_hint: Renumber positional ORDER BY after technical column removal.
notes: launch_id version_id order by renumber

vertica: DELETE FROM target_schema.partitioned_metrics WHERE event_date BETWEEN :first_date::date AND :last_date::date; INSERT INTO target_schema.partitioned_metrics SELECT * FROM stage_partitioned_metrics;
trino: SET SESSION target_catalog.insert_mode = 'OVERWRITE'; INSERT INTO target_schema.partitioned_metrics_trino SELECT * FROM stage_partitioned_metrics WHERE event_date BETWEEN CAST(:first_date AS DATE) AND CAST(:last_date AS DATE);
context_hint: For partitioned reloads, keep the write bounded by the partition column and use an optional generic overwrite session command.
notes: partition overwrite reload bounded insert

vertica: INSERT INTO target_schema.order_revenue_day SELECT :launch_id AS launch_id, :version_id AS version_id, order_id, revenue_date::date AS revenue_date, amount::numeric(18,2) AS amount FROM analytics_src.order_revenue_day_src GROUP BY 1,2,3,4,5;
trino: INSERT INTO target_schema.order_revenue_day_trino SELECT CAST(:launch_id AS BIGINT) AS launch_id, order_id, CAST(revenue_date AS DATE) AS revenue_date, CAST(amount AS DECIMAL(18,2)) AS amount FROM analytics_src.order_revenue_day_src GROUP BY 1,2,3,4;
context_hint: Drop version_id and renumber grouping ordinals after technical column removal.
notes: launch_id version_id group by 3 order by 4 renumber revenue

vertica: TRUNCATE TABLE target_schema.snapshot_users; INSERT INTO target_schema.snapshot_users SELECT user_id, event_date::date AS event_date, status FROM analytics_src.snapshot_users_src;
trino: DROP TABLE IF EXISTS target_schema.snapshot_users_trino; CREATE TABLE target_schema.snapshot_users_trino AS SELECT user_id, CAST(event_date AS DATE) AS event_date, status FROM analytics_src.snapshot_users_src;
context_hint: For an unpartitioned target, replace truncate-plus-insert with CTAS rebuild.
notes: unpartitioned refresh truncate create table as

vertica: INSERT INTO target_schema.funnel_month SELECT :launch_id AS launch_id, :version_id AS version_id, DATE_TRUNC('month', event_date)::date AS event_month, COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS users_cnt FROM analytics_src.funnel_events GROUP BY 1,2,3;
trino: INSERT INTO target_schema.funnel_month_trino SELECT CAST(:launch_id AS BIGINT) AS launch_id, CAST(DATE_TRUNC('month', event_date) AS DATE) AS event_month, COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS users_cnt FROM analytics_src.funnel_events GROUP BY 1,2;
context_hint: When dropping version_id, renumber groupings and preserve launch_id.
notes: launch_id version_id renumber monthly funnel

vertica: DELETE FROM target_schema.payment_day WHERE payment_date BETWEEN :first_date::date AND :last_date::date; INSERT INTO target_schema.payment_day SELECT payment_id, payment_date::date AS payment_date, status FROM analytics_src.payment_day_src;
trino: SET SESSION target_catalog.insert_mode = 'OVERWRITE'; INSERT INTO target_schema.payment_day_trino SELECT payment_id, CAST(payment_date AS DATE) AS payment_date, status FROM analytics_src.payment_day_src WHERE CAST(payment_date AS DATE) BETWEEN CAST(:first_date AS DATE) AND CAST(:last_date AS DATE);
context_hint: Incremental reloads should use overwrite semantics only for the affected partition window.
notes: delete truncate overwrite ctas partition reload incremental

vertica: TRUNCATE TABLE target_schema.installment_funnel; INSERT INTO target_schema.installment_funnel SELECT * FROM stage_installment_funnel;
trino: DROP TABLE IF EXISTS target_schema.installment_funnel_trino; CREATE TABLE target_schema.installment_funnel_trino AS SELECT * FROM stage_installment_funnel;
context_hint: Unpartitioned refresh targets are best rebuilt with CTAS.
notes: unpartitioned overwrite recreate truncate ctas

vertica: DELETE FROM target_schema.call_history WHERE updated_at::date >= DATE(:first_date) AND updated_at::date <= DATE(:last_date); INSERT INTO target_schema.call_history SELECT :launch_id AS launch_id, * FROM stage_call_history;
trino: SET SESSION target_catalog.insert_mode = 'OVERWRITE'; INSERT INTO target_schema.call_history_trino SELECT CAST(:launch_id AS BIGINT) AS launch_id, * FROM stage_call_history WHERE updated_at_date BETWEEN CAST(:first_date AS DATE) AND CAST(:last_date AS DATE);
context_hint: Keep overwrite inserts bounded by the partitioning date column.
notes: delete overwrite partition reload bounded call history

vertica: TRUNCATE TABLE target_schema.daily_orders; INSERT INTO target_schema.daily_orders SELECT * FROM stage_daily_orders;
trino: CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE target_schema.daily_orders_trino AS SELECT * FROM stage_daily_orders;
context_hint: Recreate a non-partitioned target instead of using truncate.
notes: unpartitioned overwrite recreate truncate ctas reload

vertica: DELETE FROM target_schema.user_orders_day WHERE event_date BETWEEN :first_date::date AND :last_date::date; INSERT INTO target_schema.user_orders_day SELECT user_id, event_date::date AS event_date, orders_cnt FROM analytics_src.user_orders_day_src;
trino: SET SESSION target_catalog.insert_mode = 'OVERWRITE'; INSERT INTO target_schema.user_orders_day_trino SELECT user_id, CAST(event_date AS DATE) AS event_date, orders_cnt FROM analytics_src.user_orders_day_src WHERE CAST(event_date AS DATE) BETWEEN CAST(:first_date AS DATE) AND CAST(:last_date AS DATE);
context_hint: Use partition-bounded overwrite for incremental reloads.
notes: delete overwrite partition reload user orders

vertica: DELETE FROM target_schema.monthly_rollup WHERE month_start BETWEEN :first_date::date AND :last_date::date; INSERT INTO target_schema.monthly_rollup SELECT month_start::date AS month_start, revenue FROM analytics_src.monthly_rollup_src;
trino: SET SESSION target_catalog.insert_mode = 'OVERWRITE'; INSERT INTO target_schema.monthly_rollup_trino SELECT CAST(month_start AS DATE) AS month_start, revenue FROM analytics_src.monthly_rollup_src WHERE CAST(month_start AS DATE) BETWEEN CAST(:first_date AS DATE) AND CAST(:last_date AS DATE);
context_hint: Bound overwrite refreshes by the partition column instead of issuing delete statements.
notes: delete truncate overwrite partition reload monthly
